referenced_documents.title: Securities and Exchange Board of India Act, 1992

reference_mentions.evidence_text: 1. SEBI (Custodian) Regulations, 1996 (Custodian Regulations) were amended vide notification dated September 18, 2025. The amendment notification, inter alia, specified provisions related to net worth, rendering of financial services, obligations and responsibilities for Custodian. In this context, the specific conditions and modalities with respect to various provisions pertaining to Custodians are being prescribed by way of this Circular.
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: Full formal title and year are present in the source paragraph.

reference_mentions.evidence_text: 1. SEBI (Custodian) Regulations, 1996 (Custodian Regulations) were amended vide notification dated September 18, 2025. The amendment notification, inter alia, specified provisions related to net worth, rendering of financial services, obligations and responsibilities for Custodian. In this context, the specific conditions and modalities with respect to various provisions pertaining to Custodians are being prescribed by way of this Circular.
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: Notification date is present, but title and identifier are absent.

reference_mentions.evidence_text: 2.1. Regulation 9 (f) of Custodian Regulations specifies as under:
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: Alias-only mention resolved from an earlier full-form citation in the same PDF.

reference_mentions.evidence_text: Provided that a custodian who is not a banking company as defined under the Banking Regulations Act, 1949 or a subsidiary/associate/joint venture of such banking company may render such financial services subject to conditions as may be specified by the Board."
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: Full formal title and year are present in the source paragraph.

reference_mentions.evidence_text: 2.1.1. The list of activities relating to rendering of financial services that can be carried out by a Custodian, in terms of the certificate granted under the Custodian Regulations shall be specified through the Custodians and DDPs Standards Setting Forum ('CDSSF'), in consultation with SEBI.
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: Alias-only mention resolved from an earlier full-form citation in the same PDF.

reference_mentions.evidence_text: 2.2. The requirements of clauses (i) and (ii) of Regulation 13 of Custodian Regulations shall not apply to a Custodian subject to such conditions as may be specified by SEBI. Accordingly, a Custodian may share manpower resources, infrastructure and systems across other financial services activities undertaken by it, subject to the following conditions:
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: Alias-only mention resolved from an earlier full-form citation in the same PDF.

reference_mentions.evidence_text: 1In terms of Regulation 7(1) of SEBI (Custodian) Regulations, 1996, Custodian shall fulfill the net worth requirements under Custodian Regulations, separately and independently, of the capital adequacy requirements, if any, for each activity undertaken by it under the relevant regulations.
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: Full formal title and year are present in the source paragraph.

match_texts: SEBI Circular no. CIR/MIRSD/5/2013 dated August 27, 2013
reference_mentions.evidence_text: 2.2.2. Custodian shall continue to adhere to general guidelines as specified in SEBI Circular no. CIR/MIRSD/5/2013 dated August 27, 2013 for dealing with conflict of interest.
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: SEBI circular identifier is present in the source paragraph.

reference_mentions.evidence_text: 3.1. In terms of SEBI Circular CIR/MIRSD/24/2011 dated 15 Dec 2011, the registered intermediaries including Custodians are allowed to outsource non- core activities. The Intermediaries, however, cannot outsource their core business activities and compliance functions.
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: SEBI circular identifier is present in the source paragraph.

match_texts: SEBI's Master Circular dated May 10, 2024 for Custodians (Custodian Master Circular)
reference_mentions.evidence_text: 4.1. Chapter III of SEBI's Master Circular dated May 10, 2024 for Custodians (Custodian Master Circular) provides specifications of vaults with Custodians.
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: Full master circular title and issue date are present in the source paragraph.

reference_mentions.evidence_text: 4.2. To facilitate ease of doing business for Custodians, provisions of the said Chapter III of the Custodian Master Circular stand replaced with the following:

reference_mentions.evidence_text: 5. Obligations and Responsibilities In order to further strengthen the compliance and governance framework for Custodians, SEBI vide Gazette notification dated September 18, 2025, inter alia, inserted Regulation 19B in the Custodian Regulations, mandating certain obligations and responsibilities. Accordingly, the following are specified:
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: Alias-only mention resolved from an earlier full-form citation in the same PDF.

reference_mentions.evidence_text: 5. Obligations and Responsibilities In order to further strengthen the compliance and governance framework for Custodians, SEBI vide Gazette notification dated September 18, 2025, inter alia, inserted Regulation 19B in the Custodian Regulations, mandating certain obligations and responsibilities. Accordingly, the following are specified:
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: Notification date is present, but title and identifier are absent.

reference_mentions.evidence_text: 5.1.2. Custodian which is a bank or subsidiary/ associate/ joint venture of a bank, may not be required to formulate separate specific committees for custody business/function and may rely on the bank wide or organization wide policies, committees, governance structures that apply across all functions of the bank, including the custodian business. However, where Custodian Regulations prescribe any additional requirements, the Custodian shall ensure that such requirements are appropriately incorporated within the relevant bank wide policies or through supplementary procedures, as necessary.
reference_mentions.confidence_reason: Alias-only mention resolved from an earlier full-form citation in the same PDF.
